chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: What is electric current?
questions.answer: Electric current is the flow of electric charges through a conductor such as a wire.

questions.question: What is an electric circuit?
questions.answer: An electric circuit is a complete path through which electric current flows.

questions.question: Name the main components of an electric circuit.
questions.answer: The main components are battery, wires, switch and an electrical device such as a bulb.

questions.question: What is a closed circuit?
questions.answer: A closed circuit is a circuit in which the path is complete and electric current flows.

questions.question: What is an open circuit?
questions.answer: An open circuit is a circuit in which the path is broken and current does not flow.

questions.question: Explain the heating effect of electric current.
questions.answer: When electric current flows through a conductor, electrical energy is converted into heat energy. This is called the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: Give two examples of appliances that use the heating effect of current.
questions.answer: Electric iron and electric heater use the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: What is an electric fuse?
questions.answer: An electric fuse is a safety device that protects electrical circuits by melting when excessive current flows.

questions.question: Why is a fuse important in electrical circuits?
questions.answer: A fuse prevents damage to electrical appliances by breaking the circuit when excessive current flows.

questions.question: Explain the magnetic effect of electric current.
questions.answer: When electric current flows through a conductor, it produces a magnetic field around it. This is called the magnetic effect of electric current.

questions.question: What is an electromagnet?
questions.answer: An electromagnet is a temporary magnet produced when electric current flows through a coil of wire.

questions.question: Give one use of an electromagnet.
questions.answer: Electromagnets are used in electric bells.

questions.question: Explain how an electromagnet works.
questions.answer: When electric current flows through a coil wrapped around an iron core, the core becomes magnetized and acts as a magnet.

questions.question: What happens when current stops flowing in an electromagnet?
questions.answer: When current stops, the electromagnet loses its magnetism.

questions.question: Explain the working of an electric bell.
questions.answer: An electric bell works using an electromagnet which attracts a metal armature and produces sound when the bell is struck repeatedly.

questions.question: Name two devices that use electromagnets.
questions.answer: Electric bells and cranes used for lifting scrap iron use electromagnets.

questions.question: Why is electricity important in daily life?
questions.answer: Electricity is important because it powers many devices such as lights, fans, heaters and electronic appliances.

questions.question: Explain why wires are used in electric circuits.
questions.answer: Wires are conductors that allow electric current to flow between different components of the circuit.

questions.question: Explain why switches are used in electric circuits.
questions.answer: Switches are used to open or close the circuit, controlling the flow of electric current.

questions.question: Explain how electricity produces heat.
questions.answer: Electric current flowing through a wire meets resistance, which converts electrical energy into heat.

questions.question: Explain how electricity produces magnetism.
questions.answer: When electric current flows through a conductor, it creates a magnetic field around the conductor.

questions.question: Why are electromagnets useful?
questions.answer: Electromagnets are useful because their magnetism can be switched on and off by controlling the electric current.

questions.question: Explain why electric bells make sound repeatedly.
questions.answer: The electromagnet repeatedly attracts and releases the armature, causing the bell to strike continuously.

questions.question: Explain why too much current can be dangerous.
questions.answer: Excessive current can cause overheating of wires, which may damage appliances or cause fires.

questions.question: Explain how electricity and magnetism are related.
questions.answer: Electric current flowing through a conductor produces a magnetic field, showing the relationship between electricity and magnetism.
